sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Stade de Reims, currently sitting at 13th in the league with 33 points, hosts Saint-Étienne, who are struggling in 17th place with 27 points. This matchup at Stade Auguste Delaune in Reims promises to be a critical encounter for both sides. With Reims looking to climb away from the mid-table mediocrity and Saint-Étienne desperate to escape the relegation zone, the stakes are high. The referee, Eric Wattellier, is known for averaging 3.1 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of this clash.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, Saint-Étienne holds a slight edge with 3 wins compared to Reims' 1 win and 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on January 4, 2025, saw Saint-Étienne triumph 3-1 at home, with Lucas Stassin earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.8. Historically, in head-to-head games, both teams have scored in 6 out of the last 7 matches, suggesting a high likelihood of goals from both sides in this game. Reims has been the first to score in all of their last 5 encounters against Saint-Étienne, which could set the tone early at home.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Reims come into this match with a streak of 5 out of 5 games having less than 2.5 goals, indicating a trend of low-scoring affairs. On the other hand, Saint-Étienne have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 6 matches and have seen over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 games. Additionally, Saint-Étienne have been the first to concede in 4 of their last 5 matches, which might give Reims an early advantage. Both teams have a tendency for cards, with Reims having 5 out of 7 games under 4.5 cards, while Saint-Étienne often see more than 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Stade de Reims
sections.text: Despite being a key player, Junya Ito, who ranks 1st in the league for key passes with 81, will be missing for Reims, which could impact their attacking creativity. However, Keito Nakamura steps up as a potential game-changer with 11 goals scored and an expected goals value of 8.89, overperforming by 2.1086. Joseph Okumu, ranking 3rd in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 9.3%, provides defensive stability with a Sofascore rating of 7.11. Another name to watch is Sergio Akieme, who has contributed 1 goal and 3 assists from defense, adding versatility to Reims’ lineup.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Saint-Étienne
sections.text: Saint-Étienne will miss Lucas Stassin, a significant loss given his 12 goals and 4 assists this season, alongside a Sofascore rating of 7.11. However, Zuriko Davitashvili remains a threat up front with 9 goals and 7 assists, overperforming his expected goals by 1.2639. Mickael Nade, ranking 4th in the league for ground duels won at 70.5%, is a defensive rock with an average rating of 6.87 and has chipped in with 1 goal and 2 assists. Irvin Cardona also adds depth to their attack, having scored 4 goals with a Sofascore rating of 6.88.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Reims have scored 32 goals this season but rank 16th in the league for total goals scored, overperforming their expected goals by 3.8371, which places them 6th in goals vs expected goals difference. They struggle with big chances created, ranking 17th with only 56, and have missed 35 big chances, though they rank 2nd for the least big chances missed. Saint-Étienne’s goal distribution shows a knack for scoring early, with 7 goals in the 1-15 minute period, accounting for 17.57% of their goals. Reims, conversely, are strong in the 46-60 minute period with 8 goals, representing 11.63% of their total, which could indicate a strong start to the second half.

sections.subtitle: Historical Player Impact
sections.text: In the last 5 matches against Reims, Mickael Nade of Saint-Étienne has been a standout performer with an average Sofascore rating of 7.3 over 2 starts. While he hasn’t scored in these encounters, he has provided 1 assist, showing his ability to influence games from the back. His presence will be crucial for Saint-Étienne as they look to exploit any defensive lapses from Reims. On the Reims side, goalkeeper Yehvann Diouf has been in fine form over the last 5 matches with an average rating of 7.78, potentially key to keeping Saint-Étienne’s attackers at bay.

sections.subtitle: Venue and Referee Impact
sections.text: Playing at Stade Auguste Delaune in Reims gives the home side a slight edge, as they aim to capitalize on familiar surroundings. The referee, Eric Wattellier, with an average of 3.1 yellow cards and 0.18 red cards per game, might maintain a tight grip on the match, especially given Reims’ record of 62 yellow cards this season, ranking 9th for the least yellow cards. Saint-Étienne’s disciplinary record could also come under scrutiny in a potentially heated affair. Expect a game with controlled aggression but a few inevitable bookings.
